mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, weak labor demand signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer discretionary spending and construction activity, confirming broad-based demand recovery that lifts equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, month-over-month expansions in the index of industrial production reflect rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, triggering short-term algorithmic buying and positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO monthly growth shows zero or negative predictive power for 5-day SPX returns during documented recovery phases, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the consumer price index above 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL monthly growth exceeds 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts household income and corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression predicting 60-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent signals sustained economic momentum that supports corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly growth rates fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns during late-cycle periods, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 for the long-only signal.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index contracts year-over-year, it indicates severe supply-side weakness and falling factory utilization, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months where INDPRO shows negative year-over-year growth.
